title: What Is a Neuron?
learning_objectives: Understand what an artificial neuron computes (weighted sum + activation)
Grasp how stacking simple neurons in layers creates intelligence
Feel awe at the elegance of simplicity becoming complexity

scenes.visual: Extreme wide shot of vast bioluminescent neural network stretching to infinity, shot on ARRI Alexa 65, Cooke S4 40mm anamorphic, volumetric cyan backlight, warm amber key light, from below, deep space atmosphere
scenes.motion: Slow dolly forward into the network, volumetric fog drifts gently

signaling: scale of the network creates awe

scenes.dialogue: Each one does something embarrassingly simple.
scenes.visual: Extreme close-up macro of a single luminous neuron node pulsing with soft warm light, shot on ARRI Alexa 65, 100mm macro lens f/2.8, shallow depth of field, warm practical light from the neuron, cool ambient fill, film grain
scenes.motion: Subtle breathing depth-of-field shift, neuron pulses gently

scenes.dialogue: But together, they create everything you have ever experienced.
scenes.visual: Medium shot pulling back from single neuron to reveal thousands firing in cascading waves of light, bioluminescent rim light, deep volumetric fog creating god rays, shot on ARRI Alexa 65, Panavision C-Series anamorphic 40mm, Kodak Vision3 500T warmth
scenes.motion: Camera pulls back revealing scale, neurons fire in cascading sequence

learning.objective: Emergence — simple parts create complex wholes

scenes.dialogue: We figured out how to copy this trick. Not perfectly. But mathematically. And it changed everything.
scenes.visual: Wide overhead shot looking down into crystalline geometric structure, an artificial neural network rendered as architectural glass and light, shot on RED V-Raptor 8K, Zeiss Master Prime 35mm, cool blue-white key light reflecting through geometric facets with warm amber accents, clean mathematical beauty
scenes.motion: Slow orbital arc circling the structure, light refracts through glass facets

scenes.dialogue: An artificial neuron takes in numbers. Multiplies each one by how important it is. Adds them up. And a tiny function decides: fire, or stay quiet. That is the entire secret.
scenes.visual: Close-up of a single water droplet falling in extreme slow motion onto a still dark surface, creating perfect concentric ripples, warm golden backlight catching the droplet edge with rim light, shot on ARRI Alexa 65, Cooke S4 75mm T2.0, Emmanuel Lubezki natural light
scenes.motion: Slow motion droplet falls, ripples expand outward in perfect circles

scenes.dialogue: One neuron draws a line. Stack thousands in layers, and the first layer learns edges. The second combines edges into shapes. The third recognizes faces. Complexity emerges from simplicity.
scenes.visual: Abstract edge patterns sharpening into geometric shapes, shapes assembling into recognizable human features emerging from pure mathematics, dramatic split lighting cool analytical teal from left and warm human amber from right, shot on RED V-Raptor 8K, Greig Fraser desaturated warmth, face resolves from wireframe to photorealistic
scenes.motion: Patterns sharpen and assemble, light shifts from cool to warm as recognition emerges
scenes.music: warm ambient, building tension, piano fragments resolve into full chord, 80 bpm

learning.objective: Layer abstraction: edges to shapes to meaning
signaling: progressive reveal mirrors neural layer learning

scenes.dialogue: Each one does its tiny job. And somehow, understanding emerges. Simplicity, stacked deep enough, becomes intelligence.
scenes.visual: Medium close-up of single neuron node glowing warmly, camera slowly pulls back to show it nested within a vast gentle constellation of others, warm golden key light dominant, soft cool fill, peaceful not overwhelming, shot on ARRI Alexa 65, Cooke S4 75mm, Roger Deakins motivated naturalism
scenes.motion: Gentle pullback revealing the constellation, warm particles drift slowly
